name: Phind
description: YC-backed Phind raised $10.4M to build an AI search engine for coders. It shut down in January 2026 after OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic added web search to their models.
story: Phind was founded in 2022 as an AI search engine specifically for developers — pulling answers from Stack Overflow, GitHub issues, and technical documentation. Backed by Y Combinator, Bessemer Venture Partners, and SV Angel, the company raised $10.4 million. For a brief window, Phind offered something genuinely useful: AI-powered developer search with source citations. Then OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic added web search capabilities to their foundation models, making a dedicated developer search engine redundant. Phind's only differentiation — a developer-focused interface — evaporated overnight. The company shut down on January 16, 2026, just over a month after its last fundraise. A textbook platform absorption.
